title: amelanchier ovalis subsp. vulgaris
intro: Amelanchier ovalis subsp. vulgaris, also known as the service tree or wild service tree, is a deciduous tree native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. Consuming the berries and flowers of the wild service tree has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its bark is also used in traditional remedies for its astringent and antiseptic properties.
